meta.title: About
meta.description: ANKA provides sustainability consultancy with a regulatory and compliance lens. We build defensible evidence, accountable ownership, and workable delivery systems across reporting, carbon, product data, and supply chain requirements—strengthened where relevant by software-enabled workflows.

hero.title: Regulation-led sustainability consultancy, strengthened with software-enabled compliance systems
hero.description: For Europe-facing organisations, we translate expectations such as CSRD/ESRS, CBAM, EUDR, and PPWR/ESPR into workable operating models—aligning data, evidence, ownership, and delivery cadence.

who.title: Who is ANKA
who.intro: ANKA is a regulatory-first sustainability advisory partner. We make sustainability requirements more manageable, defensible, and executable—by structuring data, evidence, ownership, and controls for reporting and compliance work. Where it materially improves outcomes, we reinforce this with software-enabled workflow discipline.
who.highlight3: Product evidence and environmental data structures
who.highlight4: Supply chain data and traceability systems

value.title: How we create value for organizations
value.body: We turn complex requirements into clarity at four levels: scope, data, evidence, and outputs. We define what needs to be produced, by whom, when, and against which standards—then connect reporting and compliance work to repeatable workflows. The result is not “a report”, but a system teams can run.

mission.title: Our approach and mission
mission.body: Our mission is to help organisations build a defensible, traceable, and sustainable compliance structure in areas with high regulatory pressure. We keep technical rigour and an audit mindset—while designing a practical model that can be executed inside the business. This supports better decisions, lower risk, and stronger market credibility.

aboutPage.team: Our trainers, consultants, and auditors combine deep subject expertise with disciplined field and process judgement—turning technical rigour into manageable workflows and usable outputs.
aboutPage.closing: Contact us to learn more about ANKA and explore a delivery structure that fits your priorities.

meta.title: Sector Expertise
meta.description: ANKA brings sector-aware sustainability consultancy—reporting, compliance, carbon, and product data—for industries facing European market expectations.

hero.title: Consultancy shaped around sector-specific regulatory pressure and data structures
hero.description: The same sustainability topics don’t carry the same meaning across industries. Regulatory pressure, data needs, reporting logic, and market expectations differ by sector. ANKA structures its delivery model around this context—so implementation is more workable and defensible.

textileFashion.title: Textile and Fashion
textileFashion.description: Product data, traceability, and market expectations are evolving quickly. DPP readiness and supply chain visibility are becoming decisive.

agricultureFood.title: Agriculture and Food
agricultureFood.description: Origin, traceability, and supply chain confidence are critical. Frameworks like EUDR raise the bar for data and evidence discipline.

packagingMaterials.title: Packaging, Plastics, and Materials
packagingMaterials.description: Regulations such as PPWR tighten product compliance and data requirements. Circularity, declarations, and calculations need to work as one system.

constructionMaterials.title: Construction Materials
constructionMaterials.description: LCA/EPD, product carbon footprint, and market-ready declarations are becoming standard. Product data and evidence logic must be more disciplined.

metalsIndustrial.title: Metals, Aluminium, and Industrial Manufacturing
metalsIndustrial.description: CBAM, carbon data, and reporting expectations directly influence competitiveness. Technical declarations and data discipline are critical.

automotiveMobility.title: Automotive and Mobility
automotiveMobility.description: Supply chain data and material compliance are becoming more visible across the industry. Carbon and reporting readiness is increasingly expected.

chemicals.title: Chemicals and Industrial Chemicals
chemicals.description: Product compliance, supplier information flows, and documentation discipline matter. Declarations and data logic must be clear and controlled.

tourismServices.title: Tourism and Services
tourismServices.description: For hospitality and service businesses, market expectations, performance visibility, and operational readiness need to be managed together.

contextIntro.title: Why sector context matters
contextIntro.description: The same sustainability topics don’t carry the same meaning in every sector. Regulatory pressure, data structures, customer expectations, and implementation priorities differ by industry. That’s why ANKA shapes the consultancy scope around sector context.

b1.title: Readiness for the European market
b1.description: We clarify the critical compliance topics and priorities for your sector and set a clear starting framework aligned with market expectations.

b2.title: Sector-fit data structure
b2.description: We structure product, supply chain, and reporting data to fit how your sector actually operates—strengthening traceability and control.

b3.title: A more defensible delivery model
b3.description: We strengthen evidence, documentation, and ownership logic—so work becomes more audit-ready and operationally usable.

finalCta.title: Let’s define the right sustainability framework for your sector
finalCta.description: Let’s assess your priorities, market expectations, and the consultancy structure that best fits your needs.

meta.title: Insights
meta.description: Curated perspectives from ANKA on regulation, reporting, product sustainability, compliance, and software-supported delivery.

hero.title: Selected guidance for regulation and compliance
hero.description: Selected guidance on regulatory readiness, product data, carbon, supply chain, and digital implementation—designed to be practical and reusable.

csrdMateriality.title: Where should organizations begin with CSRD and double materiality readiness
csrdMateriality.description: The foundational steps for building a more structured and more manageable corporate reporting process.
csrdMateriality.body:
CSRD readiness is not only about producing a report. If you don’t clarify what is material, which boundaries apply, and what evidence will support decisions, the process becomes unmanageable.

A strong starting frame:
- Clarify scope and expectations (ESRS, stakeholders, boundaries)
- Treat double materiality as a decision logic—not a checklist
- Design data ownership and evidence flow early

The common failure mode is running analysis and data/documentation as separate tracks. ANKA’s approach ties materiality logic to a workable data structure and delivery model.

cbamCost.title: How should CBAM data and financial impact preparation be structured
cbamCost.description: A practical framework for being better prepared not only for reporting, but also for financial exposure and data quality expectations.
cbamCost.body:
CBAM preparation has two layers: (1) data and methodology discipline for embedded emissions, and (2) visibility that translates exposure into management language.

To structure it well:
- Standardize product/installation splits and methodology choices
- Manage supplier submissions with format, quality, and traceability controls
- Make default-value boundaries explicit and governed
- Link emissions outputs to financial impact through scenarios

The goal is not “last-minute data chasing” during reporting windows, but a controlled, repeatable calculation and decision-support flow.

dppPrep.title: Critical data fields for Digital Product Passport preparation
dppPrep.description: The key areas to consider when building stronger product-level data management.
dppPrep.body:
Digital Product Passport (DPP) readiness requires treating product data not as a file, but as a governed record system with relationships and freshness controls.

Critical data areas:
- Product identity and variant/SKU logic
- Material and component structure (BOM) and sourcing information
- Compliance and declaration data (regulation, standard, verification)
- Traceability and supplier records

Success is not only listing fields—it’s building ownership, evidence links, and sharing scenarios as an executable workflow.

ppwrProductData.title: How are product data expectations changing under PPWR
ppwrProductData.description: A short perspective on why packaging and product information are becoming more important in European markets.
ppwrProductData.body:
With PPWR, packaging becomes more than design or cost—it becomes a data, declaration, and compliance obligation.

Practical implications:
- Linking packaging composition and material data to the product level
- Translating obligations into concrete data fields
- Making fee/charge logic visible and traceable

That’s why organizations need a structure that manages packaging inventory together with compliance logic and decision-support outputs.

scope3Suppliers.title: What structure is needed for collecting supplier data for Scope 3
scope3Suppliers.description: Practical recommendations for making fragmented data flows more structured and more manageable.
scope3Suppliers.body:
Scope 3 is often handled as a one-off questionnaire. The real value comes from building a durable data flow.

For an operational structure:
- Supplier segmentation and prioritization
- Field dictionary (definitions), format standards, and quality controls
- Document/evidence flow and closure discipline

The target is not just reporting—it’s making supply chain data traceable, manageable, and reusable.

reportingWorkflow.title: Why does a digital workspace matter in sustainability reporting processes
reportingWorkflow.description: How digital support improves progress tracking, data visibility, and delivery quality.
reportingWorkflow.body:
Reporting projects fall apart when data ownership, revision control, evidence links, and progress tracking are weak.

A digital workspace strengthens:
- Data collection and responsibility tracking
- Control steps and a traceable audit trail
- Repeatable reporting flows and quality standards

Digital tools are not meant to replace consulting—they should make the delivery model more controlled and defensible.

finalCta.title: Let’s review the right framework for your organisation
finalCta.description: We can quickly clarify priorities and the working model that fits your needs.

meta.title: Privacy Policy
meta.description: How ANKA Sustainability Consulting B.V. handles personal data in connection with this website and enquiries.

privacyPage.intro: This notice describes how we handle personal data when you use this website or contact ANKA Sustainability Consulting B.V. It is intended as a practical summary for a professional audience—not as an exhaustive legal memorandum.

collect.title: What we collect
collect.body: We may process contact details and message content you submit through email or contact channels, along with basic technical information needed to operate and secure the website (such as server logs where applicable).

use.title: How we use information
use.body: We use this information to respond to enquiries, maintain the site, and improve how we deliver consultancy and software-supported services—consistent with a legitimate business relationship and proportionate to the purpose.

contact.title: Contact
contact.body: For questions about this notice or your data, contact us at info@ankasustainability.com.

updates.title: Updates
updates.body: We may update this page as our practices or applicable requirements evolve. The current version will always be published here.

meta.title: Terms of Use
meta.description: Terms governing use of the ANKA Sustainability Consulting B.V. website.

termsPage.intro: By accessing this website, you agree to these terms. If you do not agree, please discontinue use of the site.

websiteUse.title: Website use
websiteUse.body: The site is provided for general business information about ANKA’s services. You agree not to misuse the website, attempt to disrupt its operation, or use automated means to extract content in a way that impairs performance or security.

intellectualProperty.title: Intellectual property
intellectualProperty.body: Text, layout, graphics, and branding elements are owned by ANKA or used under appropriate licence. Copying or redistributing content for commercial purposes without prior written consent is not permitted.

limitation.title: Limitation of reliance
limitation.body: Information on this website does not constitute legal, financial, or technical advice tailored to your organisation. Decisions should be based on professional judgement and, where appropriate, specialist counsel.

contact.title: Contact
contact.body: Questions about these terms may be directed to info@ankasustainability.com.

meta.title: Legal Notice
meta.description: Operator and legal information for the ANKA Sustainability Consulting B.V. website.

legalNoticePage.intro: This website is operated by ANKA Sustainability Consulting B.V. It presents our sustainability consultancy services and software-supported delivery capabilities in a consultancy-led model.
legalNoticePage.statement: ANKA Sustainability Consulting B.V. provides professional sustainability consultancy services. Software and digital tools are described as enabling capabilities within that consultancy relationship, not as a separate consumer product brand.

sidebar.foundationNote: This workspace is PPWR-first and designed to extend into ESPR / DPP modules without refactoring the core architecture.

overview.title: Overview
overview.subtitle: A regulation-native operational workspace for packaging compliance, evidence, responsibility mapping, and packaging cost decisions.

tables.risksSubtitle: Findings are structured outputs of the rule framework. PPWR logic will be added iteratively.
tables.scenariosSubtitle: Compare baseline vs alternative packaging and capture decision notes.

products.title: Products & SKUs
products.subtitle: Maintain product master data and link SKUs to packaging units, markets, evidence and technical file readiness.

table.title: SKU list
table.subtitle: Search, filter and open SKUs. Import/export hooks are reserved for the next package.

bom.title: Packaging BOM
bom.subtitle: Build packaging units and component structures with material and supplier links. Designed for PPWR-first checks and later DPP readiness.

table.title: BOM components
table.subtitle: A component-level view across packaging units (primary/secondary/tertiary/transport).

responsibility.title: Responsibility Mapping
responsibility.subtitle: Map economic operator responsibilities by market and supply chain context (manufacturer/importer/private label/retailer).

operator.title: Operator roles
operator.description: Define responsibility templates and apply them to SKUs and markets with traceable assumptions.

market.title: Market overlays
market.description: Attach country overlays for national nuances without hard-coding them into product pages.

audit.title: Audit-ready mapping
audit.description: Prepare exportable, customer/importer-ready responsibility output packs.

table.title: Mapping register (foundation)
table.subtitle: This foundation provides the structure; operator rules and approvals come in the next packages.

risks.title: Compliance Risks
risks.subtitle: Run structured rule checks and triage findings across markets, SKUs, packaging units, and evidence.

table.title: Findings
table.subtitle: Severity and category are rule-driven; effective-date and country overlays are first-class in the architecture.

evidence.title: Evidence Pack
evidence.subtitle: Collect supplier evidence, track review/approval state, and prepare audit-ready documentation packs.

empty.title: No evidence items yet
empty.description: Start by defining your evidence requirements and sending supplier requests. Status and workflow are built-in to support review and approval.

table.title: Evidence register
table.subtitle: Request → Received → Review → Approved flow with missing/expired handling.

scenarios.title: Scenario Comparison
scenarios.subtitle: Compare baseline packaging vs alternatives: weight reduction, material changes, compliance delta, documentation delta, and decision notes.

empty.title: No scenarios yet
empty.description: Create a scenario to compare current vs alternative packaging and capture decisions with traceable notes.

table.title: Scenarios
table.subtitle: Weight delta is computed today; compliance/documentation delta slots are wired for PPWR rule output.

technicalFile.title: Technical File
technicalFile.subtitle: Track technical documentation readiness per SKU. Evidence and rule outputs contribute to a readiness view.

table.title: Readiness by SKU
table.subtitle: Readiness is evidence-driven in this foundation; rule-driven deltas will follow.

suppliers.title: Suppliers
suppliers.subtitle: Manage suppliers, onboarding status, evidence SLA expectations, and document collection performance.

table.title: Supplier register
table.subtitle: Evidence status is linked to suppliers to support accountability and supplier portal expansion later.

markets.title: Markets
markets.subtitle: Maintain market scope and reserve explicit overlay slots for national administrative nuances and EPR variations.

table.title: Market scope
table.subtitle: Overlays attach per market without entangling the core PPWR structure.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Workspace configuration: roles, permissions, markets, rule catalogs, and effective dates.

roles.title: Roles & permissions
roles.description: Define who can manage products, BOM, evidence and technical file exports.

markets.title: Market scope
markets.description: Activate markets and attach overlay notes for local nuances.

catalogs.title: Rule catalogs
catalogs.description: PPWR-first catalogs live here. ESPR/DPP catalogs can be added later without changing the product UI structure.

effectiveDates.title: Effective dates
effectiveDates.description: Model future effective dates and transitional arrangements as first-class inputs to rule evaluation.
